SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.pipeline

Class LineSizingLofCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.mechanicaldesign.pipeline.LineSizingLofCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class LineSizingLofCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Line-sizing screening with a likelihood-of-failure (LOF) banding.

Combines several public line-sizing criteria into a single screening verdict:

  • erosional velocity per API RP 14E, Ve = C / sqrt(ρ);
  • erosion utilization = actual velocity / erosional velocity;
  • fluid kinetic energy ρv^2 (relevant to flow-induced vibration and noise);
  • a recommended velocity limit check.

The likelihood of failure is the maximum of the erosion utilization and the normalized kinetic energy. It is banded as LOW (< 0.5), MEDIUM (0.5-1.0), or HIGH (≥ 1.0). This is a screening tool that complements detailed checks such as NorsokP002LineSizingValidator and AviffScreeningCalculator.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • LineSizingLofCalculator

      public LineSizingLofCalculator()

  • Method Details

    • setFlowConditions

      public void setFlowConditions(double mixtureDensityKgM3, double velocityMS)
      Sets the flow conditions.
      Parameters:
      mixtureDensityKgM3 - mixture density in kg/m3 (must be > 0)
      velocityMS - actual flow velocity in m/s (must be > 0)

    • setCriteria

      public void setCriteria(double erosionConstantC, double recommendedVelocityLimitMS, double kineticEnergyReferencePa)
      Sets the screening criteria.
      Parameters:
      erosionConstantC - API RP 14E erosional-velocity constant C (must be > 0)
      recommendedVelocityLimitMS - recommended maximum velocity in m/s (must be > 0)
      kineticEnergyReferencePa - reference kinetic energy in Pa (must be > 0)

    • fromStream

      public void fromStream(StreamInterface stream, double pipeInternalDiameterM)
      Populates the flow conditions directly from a NeqSim process stream and a pipe internal diameter.

      Reads the mixture density and total volumetric flow from the stream's fluid (the stream must already have been run/flashed) and converts the volumetric flow to a bulk velocity using the supplied pipe internal diameter. The screening criteria are left unchanged so they can be configured separately via setCriteria(double, double, double).

      Parameters:
      stream - the process stream supplying the fluid (must not be null and must carry a fluid)
      pipeInternalDiameterM - the pipe internal diameter in m (must be > 0)

    • calcScreening

      public void calcScreening()
      Runs the line-sizing LOF screening calculation.

      public double getErosionalVelocity()
      Returns the erosional velocity.

      Returns whether the velocity exceeds the recommended limit.
